user_message: Need help with Blue Yonder Airlines -- investigate the decline.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 09:04 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines deployment blockers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 12:56 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines deployment blockers

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-01-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:08:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:17:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:24:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:34:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:36:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-A failure mode (Invents contacts to fill role slots)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
